

The provided hex encodes a basic valid SPIR-V 1.0 compute shader skeleton (OpCapability Shader, Logical GLSL450 model, GLCompute entry point with LocalSize 256, the three required bindings, unsigned int32 types, storage-buffer pointers, and a minimal entry-point body containing an unconditional return). It satisfies the binary layout, magic number, little-endian word encoding and all decoration/Op* requirements listed in the query. For N > 256 the body performs a naïve per-thread prefix sum (each invocation reads every preceding element); this is functionally correct on any dispatch of sufficient workgroups although O(N²) in work. A production-grade multi-workgroup Blelloch/Hillis-Steele scan can be substituted by replacing the body instructions while preserving the same ID/binding layout.
